shell.errorReturnDefaultRoot: Could not return to the default project root.

books.title: Books
books.intro: Each book has its own tax and currency settings. Use separate books for private and business activity; use separate Kassiber files for different clients.

create.title: New book
create.descriptionFromSource: Create a separate book in {{workspace}} from {{source}}'s tax settings.
create.descriptionInWorkspace: Create a separate book in {{workspace}} and choose its region and accounting method.
create.descriptionDefault: Create a separate book using default settings.
create.copyHint: Copying settings only copies tax policy, currency, holding period, and lot selection. Wallets, accounts, and transactions stay in the original books.
create.austrianMethodNote: Austrian books use the moving-average method (gleitender Durchschnittspreis) by default. Other methods are available but not standard for Austria.

renameProfile.title: Book settings
renameProfile.description: Rename the book or change its region and accounting method. Changing the region or method reprocesses journals so reports recompute.
renameProfile.austrianMethodNote: Austrian books use the moving-average method (gleitender Durchschnittspreis) by default. Other methods are available but not standard for Austria.

switchRegion.title: Switch region?
switchRegion.body: Change this book from {{from}} to {{to}}, resetting the accounting method to {{method}}.
switchRegion.warning: All journals are reprocessed so reports recompute.

renameWorkspace.title: Edit book set name
renameWorkspace.description: Rename this book set. Books, wallets, and transactions stay unchanged.

createWorkspace.title: New book set
createWorkspace.description: Create a separate top-level set in this local database, then add its first book. Most users only need one.

start.title: Open your local books

createNew.title: Create new books
createNew.body: Create a new local Kassiber database with sensible defaults.

openExisting.title: Open existing books
openExisting.body: Open a Kassiber state root or data folder and choose local books.
openExisting.tooltipUnavailable: Opening existing books is available in the desktop app

start.privacyNote: Kassiber stores watch-only accounting data locally. Private keys never enter the app.

localDatabase.title: One local database
localDatabase.body: ~/.kassiber by default, or an imported Kassiber folder.

encrypted.title: Encrypted by default
encrypted.body: SQLCipher protects real books at rest.

importLocal.title: Import stays local
importLocal.body: Existing books are listed after the selected folder opens.

import.title: Open existing books
import.rightBody: The desktop daemon now points at this selected data root. Once a book opens, the same local database powers the rest of the app.
import.empty: No books were found in this local data root.

default.title: Use built-in public backends
default.description: Start quickly with the bundled Explorer API, Electrum / Fulcrum, and Liquid endpoints. You can replace them later.

custom.title: Use a custom sync backend
custom.description: Point Kassiber at a clearnet or .onion Explorer API (Esplora/mempool), Electrum / Fulcrum, Bitcoin Core RPC, or Liquid endpoint.

skip.title: Skip connections for now
skip.description: Continue with manual imports only. Watch-only refresh can be configured from Settings later.

connections.protocolDescription: Payment providers and file imports are configured later from Connections.
connections.displayNameDescription: A short label shown in Settings and connection refresh screens.
connections.useSslDescription: Use TLS on the Electrum connection; common servers listen on 50002.
connections.trustSslDescription: Use only for a server you operate or have verified out of band.
connections.certificateIgnored: Ignored while 'Trust self-signed certificate' is on.
connections.certificateSystemStore: Leave empty to use the system certificate store.
connections.useProxyDescription: Deliberate per-connection routing. Only this backend uses the configured Tor/SOCKS/HTTP proxy; other connections keep their own routing.
connections.onionDetectedBody: Kassiber filled the standard local Tor SOCKS proxy for this connection only. Keep Tor running at 127.0.0.1:9050, or edit the proxy below; other connections keep their own routing.
connections.testHint: Optional — check the endpoint is reachable.
connections.credentialsWarning: Do not paste API tokens, RPC passwords, cookies, or bearer headers here. Credentials should be added only after the encrypted database is open.
connections.skipWarningTitle: Watch-only refresh will not be ready.
connections.skipWarningBody: You can still import files, but address discovery, wallet refresh, and node-backed history remain disabled until a backend is configured.
connections.skipAck: I understand sync needs a backend later.
connections.skipAckDescription: Settings can add Bitcoin or Liquid sync backends after onboarding.

local.title: Use a local endpoint
local.description: Default to Ollama on this machine. You can point this at another local OpenAI-compatible server.

remote.title: Use a remote provider
remote.description: Prepare an OpenAI-compatible endpoint or Claude/Codex CLI. Prompts and accounting context may leave this device.

disabled.title: Disable AI for now
disabled.description: Hide the Assistant and floating chat. You can turn AI back on later in Settings.

ai.providerPrivacyDescription: TEE means the provider claims a trusted-execution path; Kassiber still treats it as off-device.
ai.providerNameDescription: A short label shown in AI provider settings.
ai.baseUrlDescription: Use an OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint or a supported CLI locator.
ai.remoteAck: I understand prompts may leave this device.
ai.remoteAckDescription: Accounting context, labels, notes, backend hostnames, and report details can be sensitive.
ai.disabledNote: This hides the Assistant screen and floating chat after onboarding. You can enable AI features later in Settings.

sync.title: How syncing works
sync.lead: A sync backend discovers your watch-only addresses and refreshes balances and history from the network. Kassiber stays watch-only — private keys never enter the app.

default.title: Built-in public backends
default.body: Bundled Explorer API, Electrum / Fulcrum, and Liquid endpoints. Fast start, but the operator can still see your address queries — use Tor/VPN or run your own if privacy matters.

custom.title: Custom sync backend
custom.body: Point at your own node or a trusted server. Best privacy and sovereignty; you manage the endpoint.

skip.title: Skip for now
skip.body: Manual imports only. No address discovery or refresh until you add a backend from Settings.

sync.footer: Whichever you pick, queries reveal which addresses you watch to that server — running your own keeps it private. Credentials are added later, after the encrypted database is open.

ai.title: How the assistant works
ai.lead: The assistant helps categorize transactions, draft notes, and answer questions about your books. It is optional and can be changed anytime in Settings.

local.title: Local endpoint
local.body: Runs against a model on this machine (Ollama by default). Prompts and accounting context stay on-device.

remote.title: Remote provider
remote.body: An OpenAI-compatible endpoint or Claude/Codex CLI. Prompts and accounting context may leave this device.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.body: Hides the Assistant and floating chat. You can turn AI back on later in Settings.

ai.footer: No API keys or secrets are collected during setup. Accounting context — labels, notes, hostnames, report details — can be sensitive; keep that in mind for remote providers.

essentials.title: Set up your books
essentials.booksNameDescription: Shown in the app header. Add separate private, business, or client books later.
essentials.jurisdictionDescriptionAt: Austrian crypto-tax workflow — EUR and moving-average rules apply.
essentials.jurisdictionDescriptionGeneric: Country-neutral books with FIFO, LIFO, HIFO, or LOFO lot selection.
essentials.movingAverageNote: Older holdings can be marked later per wallet.

security.title: Protect your books

encrypt.title: Encrypt these books
encrypt.description: Recommended for real data. The local SQLite database opens only with your passphrase.

plaintext.title: Leave it unencrypted
plaintext.description: Useful for throwaway evaluation only. Anyone with disk access can read the database.

security.passphraseNote: Sent only to the local daemon to unlock the database — never stored in the UI.
security.recoveryAck: I understand there is no passphrase recovery path.
security.recoveryAckDescription: If the passphrase is lost, the SQLCipher database cannot be opened.
security.touchIdDescription: Store the passphrase in the macOS Keychain so you can unlock with Touch ID instead of typing it. Remove it anytime in Settings.
security.migrateCredentials: Move credentials from backends.env into the encrypted DB.
security.migrateCredentialsDescription: Useful when upgrading an existing CLI setup. New books can leave this checked safely.
security.plaintextAck: I understand plaintext mode is not for real wallet data.
security.plaintextAckDescription: Balances, addresses, tags, and backend metadata are readable by anything with disk access.

review.title: Review setup
review.intro: Confirm the choices that affect local storage, network visibility, and whether data can leave this machine.

books.note: Creates one local books set with one active book.

tax.noteAt: Austrian reporting currency and method are jurisdiction defaults.

sync.noteSkip: No address discovery or watch-only refresh until Settings adds a backend.
sync.noteDefaultLoading: Backend addresses are loading from Kassiber.
sync.noteDefault: Public operators can see your address queries. Use Tor or your own node if that matters.
sync.noteCustomProxy: Custom backend routed through its configured proxy only.
sync.noteCustom: Custom backend. Use your own infrastructure or a trusted operator.

ai.noteDisabled: No assistant calls during normal app use.
ai.noteRemote: Remote provider can receive selected book context after consent.
ai.noteLocal: Local provider endpoint; book context stays on your machine unless configured otherwise.

storage.noteEncrypted: Passphrase required. Touch ID {{touchId}}. No recovery path.
storage.notePlaintext: Evaluation only. Anyone with disk access can read wallet data.

secrets.noteEncrypted: Existing backends.env credentials migrate if present; new secrets are added later.
secrets.noteNone: Backend API keys, cookies, and passwords are added later.
